solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def kthFactor(self, n: int, k: int) -> int:
        factors = []
        for i in range(1, n + 1):
            if n % i == 0:
                factors.append(i)
                if len(factors) > k:
                    break
        return factors[k - 1] if len(factors) >= k else -1
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <Iterates through all integers up to n, appending factors to a list. Returns the k-th factor if it exists, otherwise -1. Efficient for small n but not optimal for large n.>
